author: mark
text:
Document multi-repo operational model and tracker-proxy bridge

Add two H3 subsections to the Multi-repo section of SKILL.md so agents have an operational model, not just opt-in mechanics.

Working in a multi-repo hub spells out that balls has no notion of which code repo a ball belongs to (the shared balls/tasks branch is flat and bl prime lists everything), so participants need conventions. Four rules: claim from the clone whose source the ball touches (worktrees land under the current clone); tag every ball with its target repo since there is no built-in repo field; model cross-cutting work as an umbrella parent plus per-repo children; the hub itself is typically a bare repo carrying only the balls/tasks ref, though a real code repo can double as the hub.

Bridging to an external tracker documents the intended pattern for integrating Jira / Linear / GitHub Issues without N copies of the plugin config: install the plugin in one participating clone (the bridge) and let the other repos proxy through the shared state branch. Includes an ASCII topology diagram. Explains why one bridge beats per-repo plugins (single credential store, single sync schedule, single mirroring policy) and notes that the bridge's BALLS_IDENTITY needs to be stable since it shows up on every mirrored ball.
